owner.description: engineer owns Worker config/source/migration files like package.json, tsconfig.json when TypeScript is used, wrangler.jsonc, wrangler.toml, src/**, workers/**, and migrations/**. In a brand-new Worker project, the root scaffold task owns package.json, .gitignore, wrangler.jsonc, and the Worker entrypoint together so Wrangler dry-run validation can run from the first build slice. Worker config tasks should define env.staging and env.production, mirroring required bindings and vars because Wrangler environments do not inherit them. New Worker package scripts should use wrangler dev --env staging and wrangler deploy --env production. TypeScript Worker scaffolds should use Wrangler-generated worker-configuration.d.ts types, not hand-written runtime types. designer owns static UI files like public/index.html, public/styles.css, public/app.js, and assets/**.

source_task_id.description: When a task is a generated or reviewed slice of a larger task, the original task id whose acceptance contract is being preserved.

source_acceptance_criteria.description: Original acceptance criteria carried forward when a task is split or revised. These are first-class contracts and must not disappear during planning or review.

owned_surfaces.description: Concrete repo paths this task will change, for example package.json, wrangler.jsonc, wrangler.toml, src/index.js, workers/tally.js, public/settings.html, migrations/0001_schema.sql. Wildcards such as src/**/*.ts, src/storage/*.ts, public/**, or src/** are not allowed. Use ['unknown: <reason>'] only when genuinely unknown; conceptual labels are not allowed. In a bare Worker folder, root scaffold ownership must include package.json, .gitignore, wrangler.jsonc, and at least one concrete Worker source input such as src/index.js or workers/app.js before runtime/source/static asset/migration tasks; include tsconfig.json only for TypeScript Worker source, configure package scripts to run wrangler dev --env staging, wrangler deploy --env production, and wrangler types before tsc, and define env.staging/env.production for staging-aware local validation and human-approved Wrangler production deploys.

open_decisions.description: Only genuine blockers. Each entry should use: Topic: ... | Why it matters: ... | Options considered: ... | Follow-up impact: ...
